summary: Matched six previously undecompiled stage functions across grcastle, grcorneria, grrcruise, grzebesroute, groldyoshi, and grpushon. The PR replaced UNK prototypes with typed signatures, refined several stage data/ground-variable layouts, and decomp.dev reported all six functions at 100.00% with +680 matched bytes and +0.02% overall matched code.

key_files.role: Matched fn_801CFB68 and split grCastleParams.x4 from f32 into s16 x4 plus s16 x6.
Added Item_GObj forward include and typed fn_801CFB68 prototype.
Matched fn_801E12D4 beam-damage callback and added static grCn_804D69A0.
Matched fn_80201BE0 display wrapper with camera/gamemode guards.
Replaced fn_80201BE0 UNK prototype with void fn_80201BE0(HSD_GObj*, s32).
Matched fn_8020B4D8 collision callback using PAD_STACK and stage_info flag update.
Added forward includes and typed fn_8020B4D8 callback prototype.
Matched fn_8020F2A8 collision/joint callback and declared grOy_JointPair lookup table.
Added forward includes and typed fn_8020F2A8 callback prototype.
Matched grPushOn_80219230 and refined grPushOn data layout with a key/value lookup table.
Added grOldYoshi_Entry, grOldYoshi_GroundVars, and the oldyoshi member in Ground.gv.

what_changed: Implemented fn_801CFB68 in grcastle: sets gp->gv.pura.xC4 to 4 and, when ftLib_80086960(gobj) is nonzero, calls ftLib_80086A4C with grCs_804D6970->x4 cast to f32.
Adjusted grCastleParams layout by changing x4 from f32 to s16 x4 plus s16 x6, which also improved grcastle .sdata2 matching per the bot report.

Implemented fn_801E12D4 in grcorneria: accumulates beam damage into gr->gv.corneria.x114, prints OSReport("*** Beam Damage! %f\n", damage), compares against M2C_FIELD(grCn_804D69A0, f32*, 0x38), sets x108 to 4, and updates left/right cannon materials.
Added static void* grCn_804D69A0 in grcorneria, contributing to a .sbss matching improvement reported by decomp.dev.
Implemented fn_80201BE0 in grrcruise: skips display if gm_801A45E8(1), gm_801A45E8(2), or Camera_8003010C() is nonzero; otherwise calls grDisplay_801C5DB0(gobj, pass).
Implemented fn_8020B4D8 in grzebesroute: checks coll->x34_flags.b1234, verifies Ground_801C57A4() equals coll->x0_gobj, checks kind == 1, then ORs stage_info.flags with 0x10.
Implemented fn_8020F2A8 in groldyoshi: for collision flag b1234 == 1, compares joint_id against grOy_803E6574 entries and sets gp->gv.oldyoshi.entries[idx].b4 when a match is found.
Added grOldYoshi_Entry and grOldYoshi_GroundVars in gr/types.h, including bitfields b0123, b4, b567 and entries[3], then wired it into the Ground.gv union.
Implemented grPushOn_80219230: scans grPushOn_804D6AB8->x10c key/value entries until key -1 or count 0x21, returns the matching value, and asserts with __assert("grpushon.c", 0x35DU, "0") on failure.
Refined grpushon static data layout from x1c[0x21] to x1c[0x1E] plus x10c[0x21] lookup entries.
Updated headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete prototypes for the matched functions and added needed forward/include dependencies.
smart_moves: Matched several small callbacks by first correcting the surrounding data layout: grCastleParams, grPushOn_804D6AB8, and Ground.gv.oldyoshi were all adjusted before or alongside the function bodies.
Kept unresolved data access explicit with M2C_FIELD(grCn_804D69A0, f32*, 0x38) rather than inventing an unsupported full struct for Corneria parameters.
Used a stage-specific Ground.gv struct for Old Yoshi so fn_8020F2A8 could write a named bitfield instead of raw pointer arithmetic.
Preserved codegen-sensitive shapes: nested conditionals, assignment in a while condition, pointer pre-increment in the Old Yoshi lookup, and a PAD_STACK(16) in Zebes Route.
Added only the necessary forward/include headers to support typed prototypes, avoiding broad header churn.
Verified the match with both the PR summary's ninja/fuzzy-match claim and the decomp.dev bot report showing six 100.00% new matches.
decomp_lessons: For stage decomp, tiny callback bodies often depend on exact data-section and struct layout; matching may require fixing globals, arrays, and union members, not just writing the control flow.

When a callback signature repeats across stages, replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with the concrete argument pattern once evidence is clear: Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32 appears in both Old Yoshi and Zebes Route here.
Bitfield names like b1234 and b4 are acceptable transitional documentation when they preserve observed layout and allow matched field writes.
Use M2C_FIELD for an unknown parameter blob when the access offset is known but the full type is not yet justified.
Compiler-shape helpers such as PAD_STACK and exact assert literals/line numbers can be necessary even in logically simple functions.
Static globals and data declarations can improve non-code section matching, as shown by decomp.dev improvements in grcastle .sdata2, grcorneria .sbss, and grpushon .sdata.
naming_conventions: Address-derived names such as fn_801CFB68, fn_801E12D4, fn_80201BE0, fn_8020B4D8, and fn_8020F2A8 were retained where no semantic name was introduced.
Stage-prefixed symbols stayed consistent: grCs for castle, grCn for corneria, grOy for old Yoshi, grPushOn for pushon, grRCruise for rainbow cruise, and grZebesRoute for zebes route.
New temporary structs were namespaced by subsystem/stage: grPushOn_Entry, grPushOn_Lookup, grOldYoshi_Entry, grOldYoshi_GroundVars, and grOy_JointPair.
Unknown fields used offset/bitfield-style names such as x1c, x10c, x114, b1234, and b4 rather than speculative semantic names.
Headers kept the existing address-comment format while replacing unknown prototypes with concrete typed prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: fn_8020B4D8 used PAD_STACK(16), indicating stack shape was needed for the match.
fn_8020B4D8 used nested if statements rather than collapsing to a single compound condition, preserving likely branch order.
fn_8020F2A8 used pointer-sensitive lookup code with (++p)->x and (p + 1)->x to match the original codegen.
grPushOn_80219230 used while (i < 0x21 && (key = ... ) != -1) with a sentinel key of -1 and an exact __assert file/line/string.
fn_801E12D4 used M2C_FIELD on grCn_804D69A0 at offset 0x38 for the damage threshold instead of over-typing an unknown blob.
grPushOn_804D6AB8 layout was reshaped so x10c begins after x1c[0x1E], enabling the lookup-table access pattern.
grCastleParams changed x4 from f32 to s16 and added x6, then fn_801CFB68 explicitly cast x4 to f32 for ftLib_80086A4C.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported six new 100.00% matches: fn_801E12D4, fn_8020F2A8, grPushOn_80219230, fn_80201BE0, fn_8020B4D8, and fn_801CFB68.
The bot reported overall matched code 55.96% with +0.02% and +680 bytes, plus unmatched-section improvements in grcastle .sdata2, grcorneria .sbss, and grpushon .sdata.
The PR summary states ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent is 100.0 for all six functions.
follow_up_queries: Find other stage collision callbacks with signature Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32 that still use UNK_PARAMS.
Search for remaining Ground.gv stage-specific raw accesses that could become typed GroundVars structs like grOldYoshi_GroundVars.
Investigate grCn_804D69A0 parameter blob around offset 0x38 to replace M2C_FIELD with a justified Corneria params struct when more fields are known.
Search grpushon data users to validate the x1c[0x1E] plus x10c[0x21] layout and name the key/value meanings.
Look for other small stage display callbacks guarded by gm_801A45E8 and Camera_8003010C before calling grDisplay_801C5DB0.
